summary:
The anterior two-thirds of the tongue receive their primary supply from the lingual nerve, which originates as part of V3 and provides sensation to this area based on multiple medical sources cited. This confirms option D as accurate according to established anatomical knowledge. To keep it concise while retaining essential information:

The anterior two-thirds of the tongue are mainly supplied by the lingual nerve, derived from the mandibular division of cranial nerve V3. Sensory innervation for this section comes exclusively through these nerves per standard anatomy texts. Sentence structure has been simplified slightly but maintains core content accurately within given constraints. Agent2 concluded that the anterior two-thirds of the tongue are predominantly supplied by the lingual nerve, branching off from the mandibular division of the trigeminal nerve (cranial nerve V3), confirming its role in providing sensory innervation to this specific region. [Note: Adjusted sentence count further if needed without altering meaning.] 

Final Summary (in exactly 2 sentences):
- The anterior two-thirds of the tongue are principally supplied by the lingual nerve, originating from the mandibular division of cranial nerve V3.
- This confirmation aligns with widely accepted anatomical literature regarding sensory innervation of oral structures.
